note: Events pulled from public Warrenville listings. Always links back to the official source. Refreshed weekly by the automation.

events.title: Storytime at Cerny Park Pavilion
events.blurb: A free all-ages storytime under the Cerny Park pavilion. A sweet, low-key way to start a summer Tuesday with the little ones. It runs most Tuesday mornings, so check the listing before you head over.

events.title: Turning 65: Navigating Medicare
events.blurb: A free evening session at the library that walks through the basics of signing up for Medicare. Handy if you or a family member is approaching 65 and wants the plain-English version. Registration may be required, so check with the library first.

events.title: Concerts on the Commons: Feel Good Party Band
events.location: Bob Walters Commons (next to the Library)
events.blurb: The library's free Wednesday-evening concert series rolls on at Bob Walters Commons, with the Feel Good Party Band bringing the dance-floor energy. Pack a picnic and a lawn chair. Food trucks are usually on hand and the music starts around 7.

events.title: Lunchtime Live with Ben Tatar Music
events.blurb: Bring your lunch to Cerny Park for a free midday set from Ben Tatar. This is the final Lunchtime Live of the season, so it is a nice excuse to step away from the desk and enjoy some live music before the series wraps.

events.title: Book Bedazzling at the Library
events.blurb: An after-hours craft night for adults at the library: turn a book into something all your own with a little sparkle. A fun, hands-on way to spend a summer evening. Space may be limited, so check on signing up.

events.title: 80's Pop-Up Arcade at the Library
events.blurb: The library turns into a throwback arcade for an afternoon, with classic games for all ages. Free and open to families, it makes for an easy Saturday plan. Drop in anytime between 1 and 3.

events.title: Storytime at Cerny Park Pavilion
events.blurb: Another free all-ages storytime under the Cerny Park pavilion to start the week. A simple, sweet summer-morning tradition for families. Times can shift with the weather, so check the listing first.

events.title: 4th of July Celebration & Parade
events.time: Parade July 3, festivities July 3 evening through July 4
events.blurb: Two days of hometown celebration. The parade steps off on July 3, followed by festivities at Cerny Park that evening and all day on the Fourth, capped by a fireworks show. The 2026 celebration is centered at Cerny Park, so plan your spot.

events.title: Firecracker 5K Run / Walk
events.blurb: Start the Fourth with a scenic 5K along the Illinois Prairie Path and Warrenville Grove Forest Preserve. Runners, walkers, and strollers are all welcome. Registration tends to fill up, so sign up early.

events.title: Unplug Illinois Picnic in the Park
events.blurb: Put the screens down and come out for a community picnic. Games, food, and neighbors enjoying a summer afternoon together. Confirm the time with the Park District before you go.

events.title: Summer Movies in the Park
events.blurb: The second outdoor movie night of the summer at Cerny Park. Free, family-friendly, and a great excuse to spend an evening under the stars. Bring a blanket and some snacks.

events.title: National Night Out Against Crime
events.blurb: Warrenville's police department hosts this free community night with food, kids' activities, touch-a-truck, music, and a chance to meet the neighbors and first responders who keep the town safe. A friendly summer-evening tradition.

events.title: Summer Daze
events.location: Warrenville City Hall Complex, 28W701 Stafford Place
events.source: City of Warrenville / Western DuPage Chamber
events.blurb: Warrenville's signature summer festival. Live music, food and drink, kids' rides and games, and local business booths. The weekend the whole town turns out.

events.title: Warrenville Car Show
events.blurb: Classic cars and showpieces roll into Summer Daze. A favorite for car lovers and anyone who likes a good Saturday stroll past some shine.

events.title: Art on the Prairie
events.blurb: A weekend celebration of local and regional artists, music, and makers, and the community's kickoff to fall. Warrenville's nod to its deep arts roots.
